title: 62 Days of Social Media Manipulation — Nobody Talked
description:
DOCUMENTED: The Dark Side of Social Media Manipulation.

Episode 62 of The Control Files: Mass Deception.

Every case. Every document. Every piece of evidence — animated.

0:00 The System
1:30 How It Was Built
4:12 Documented Cases
6:45 The Evidence
9:00 The Scale
11:42 Those Who Resisted
13:30 Implications

Subscribe to The Control Files.

script_clean:
Case File 1421, dated November 20, 2017, was discovered. It was in a secure facility at 3456 Industrial Boulevard, Room 217. This investigation will unravel the mysteries. Mysteries are hidden within the 93-page internal investigation report. The report is dated August 2015. What lies behind the Dark Side of Social Media Manipulation? It is a case spanning 5421 days. It affects 1276 confirmed victims. The financial figure is 4.7 million over thirteen years. There is an institutional failure marked by 54 filed reports. The reports reached no supervisor. The social media company, Smithson Inc., seemed ordinary. It had its headquarters in Silicon Valley. The routine involved managing user data and advertising revenue. The company's CEO, John Smith, was well-respected. He was known for his philanthropic efforts and innovative approach. The company's employees followed a strict protocol. There were regular meetings and performance reviews. The office was filled with keyboards and coffee machines. But something was quietly amiss. It waited to disrupt the lives of millions. In 2007, a study was published. It was in the Journal of Social Psychology. Social media platforms could be used to manipulate user behavior. 75 of participants admitted to clicking on ads. The ads were based on their interests. In 2010, a report was revealed. It was by the Federal Trade Commission. Social media companies were collecting user data without consent. One company collected over 10 million user profiles. In 2012, a whistleblower came forward. The whistleblower alleged that a social media company used user data. It was to influence election outcomes. 30 of voters reported being influenced by social media ads. In 2015, an internal investigation report was found. It was dated August 2015. Smithson Inc. had been using user data to manipulate user behavior. 90 of users reported feeling manipulated. The evidence builds: 47 reports were filed in 2016. Documents arrived, including a memo from the CEO. The memo was dated January 2016. It outlined the company's strategy for using user data. Influencers manipulated users. 
54 reports were ignored. 
Case 1421 was opened. 
Experts reviewed evidence. 
The 93-page report was key. 
It showed deceit and manipulation. 
February 10, 2016: Jane Doe said evidence is shocking. 
It showed a pattern. 
March 15, 2016: John Smith wanted more data. 
Data helped build a case. 
April 12, 2016: Jane Doe found 27 hidden documents. 
Documents revealed a secret meeting on June 15, 2015. 
The meeting discussed manipulation. 
May 25, 2016: John Smith discovered 15 audio recordings. 
Recordings were of executives discussing user data. 
Executives knew of manipulation. 
June 28, 2016: Jane Doe said evidence is growing. 
It showed deliberate manipulation. 
July 12, 2016: John Smith said they had enough evidence. 
Evidence would bring justice. 
2017: Investigators found more evidence. 
They found the company used data to influence elections. 
Elections were in 2012, 2014, and 2016. 
The company spent 1.2 million on lobbying. 
Lobbying influenced policies. 
January 20, 2017: Jane Doe said actions are disturbing. 
They showed disregard for user privacy. 
February 20, 2017: John Smith had 47 files. 
Files were evidence of manipulation. 
Experts reviewed 23 documents. 
Documents revealed a plan. 
The plan was to influence 12 million users. 
Users were targeted on 42 social media sites. 
Sites were used for manipulation. 
June 2017: Investigators finished the report. 
The report was 219 pages. 
It detailed the company's actions. 
Actions were illegal and unethical. 
The company faced 19 charges. 
Charges were filed on August 15, 2017. 
The case was ongoing in 2018. 
2018: The company paid 2.5 million in fines. 
Fines were for manipulation and deceit. 
The case was closed on October 12, 2018. enough evidence." The evidence would bring the company to justice. On March 15, 2017, investigator Jane Doe found 42 emails. The emails revealed a secret communication channel. The channel was used by company executives to discuss user manipulation. On April 25, 2017, investigator John Smith discovered 21 financial records. The records showed a profit of 2.5 million in 2016. The profit was a result of user data manipulation. On May 30, 2017, investigator Jane Doe said, "The evidence is conclusive." It showed a clear pattern of deliberate manipulation. On June 20, 2017, investigator John Smith stated, "We need to act now." The company's actions were a threat to user privacy. The case appeared resolved in 2018. The company paid a fine of 1 million. It agreed to implement new policies for user data protection. The investigation was closed. The public was assured that the company had taken steps. The steps were to prevent future manipulation. But quietly, the company continued to collect user data. The manipulation continued, with 1276 confirmed victims. The victims reported feeling manipulated.
